Functions for initializing and deinitializing GAP LE in Bluetooth communication.

# Purpose
This C header file declares two functions, [`gap_le_init`](<#gap_le_init>) and [`gap_le_deinit`](<#gap_le_deinit>), which are likely used to initialize and deinitialize a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) Generic Access Profile (GAP) interface. The `#pragma once` directive ensures the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions. The inclusion of `<stdint.h>` suggests that the functions may use standard integer types defined in this header, although no specific types are used in the function declarations themselves. This file is part of a larger codebase that manages BLE operations, and it provides the necessary interface for setting up and tearing down the GAP layer.
